sectiondef.header: Macro for Maximum number of Counters
Indices for Metric Counters and Sampled Metric Coounters used with
Indices for Incrementers and Sampled Incrementers used with
Macros for Maximum number of Agents
Macros for Flags in Flag Enable Control Register
Macros for Read/Write Latency Start and End points

briefdescription.para: Address Issue as start point for Latency calculation.
Address Acceptance as start point for Latency calculation.
Last Read as end point for Latency calculation.
Last Write as end point for Latency calculation.
First Read as end point for Latency calculation.
First Write as end point for Latency calculation.

sectiondef.header: Register offsets of AXIMONITOR in the Device Config

description.para: The following constants provide access to each of the registers of the AXI PERFORMANCE MONITOR device.

sectiondef.header: AXI Monitor Sample Interval Control Register mask(s)

briefdescription.para: Load the Sample Interval Register Value into the counter.

sectiondef.header: Interrupt Status/Enable Register Bit Definitions and Masks

memberdef.name: XAPM_IXR_ALL_MASK
memberdef.initializer: (XAPM_IXR_SIC_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_GCC_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_FIFO_FULL_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC0_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC1_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC2_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC3_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC4_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC5_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC6_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC7_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC8_OVERFLOW_MASK | \ XAPM_IXR_MC9_OVERFLOW_MASK)

briefdescription.para: Enable External trigger to start event Log
Enable External trigger to start Metric Counters.

sectiondef.header: AXI Monitor ID Mask Register mask(s)

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each System Monitor/ADC device in the system.
This table contains configuration information for each System Monitor/ADC device in the system.
This function initializes a specific XAxiPmon device/instance.

detaileddescription.para: Functions in xaxipmon.c.
This function must be called prior to using the AXI Performance Monitor device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
points to the XAxiPmon device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XAxiPmon_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XAxiPmon_CfgInitialize() API.

briefdescription.para: This function resets all Metric Counters and Sampled Metric Counters of AXI Performance Monitor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function resets Global Clock Counter of AXI Performance Monitor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function resets Streaming FIFO of AXI Performance Monitor.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u8 IncrementerNum, u16 RangeUpper, u16 RangeLower)
memberdef.name: XAxiPmon_SetIncrementerRange

briefdescription.para: This function sets Ranges for Incrementers depending on parameters passed.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
specifies the Incrementer for which Ranges need to be set
specifies the Upper limit in 32 bit Register
specifies the Lower limit in 32 bit Register

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Ranges of Incrementers Registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
specifies the Incrementer for which Ranges need to be returned.
specifies the user reference variable which returns the Upper Range Value of the specified Incrementer.
specifies the user reference variable which returns the Lower Range Value of the specified Incrementer.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Sample Interval Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Sample Interval value to be set

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of Sample Interval Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is a pointer where the Sample Interval Counter value is returned.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u8 Slot, u8 Metrics, u8 CounterNum)
memberdef.name: XAxiPmon_SetMetrics

briefdescription.para: This function sets Metrics for specified Counter in the corresponding Metric Selector Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the slot ID for which specified counter has to be connected.
is one of the Metric Sets. User has to use XAPM_METRIC_SET_* macros in xaxipmon.h for this parameter
is the Counter Number. The valid values are 0 to 9.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if Success XST_FAILURE if Failure

briefdescription.para: This function returns Metrics in the specified Counter from the corresponding Metric Selector Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Counter Number. The valid values are 0 to 9.
is a reference parameter from application where metrics of specified counter is filled.
is a reference parameter in which slot Id of specified counter is filled

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if Success XST_FAILURE if Failure

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the Global Clock Counter Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the user space pointer with which upper 32 bits of Global Clock Counter has to be filled
is the user space pointer with which lower 32 bits of Global Clock Counter has to be filled

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the Metric Counter Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

is the number of the Metric Counter to be read. Use the XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER* defines for the counter number in xaxipmon.h. The valid values are 0 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_0) to 47(XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_47).

simplesect.para: RegValue is the content of specified Metric Counter.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the Sampled Metric Counter Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

is the number of the Sampled Metric Counter to read. Use the XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER* defines for the counter number in xaxipmon.h. The valid values are 0 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_0) to 47(XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_47).

simplesect.para: RegValue is the content of specified Sampled Metric Counter.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the Incrementer Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the number of the Incrementer register to read.Use the XAPM_INCREMENTER_* defines for the Incrementer number.The valid values are 0 (XAPM_INCREMENTER_0) to 9 (XAPM_INCREMENTER_9).
is the number of the specified Incrementer register

simplesect.para: RegValue is content of specified Metric Incrementer register.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the contents of the Sampled Incrementer Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the number of the Sampled Incrementer register to read.Use the XAPM_INCREMENTER_* defines for the Incrementer number.The valid values are 0 (XAPM_INCREMENTER_0) to 9 (XAPM_INCREMENTER_9).
is the number of the specified Sampled Incrementer register

simplesect.para: RegValue is content of specified Sampled Incrementer register.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Software-written Data Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Software written Data.

briefdescription.para: This function returns contents of Software-written Data Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the following in the AXI Performance Monitor:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

is a value to write to the flag enables register defined by XAPM_FEC_OFFSET. It is recommended to use the XAPM_FEC_*_MASK mask bits to generate. A value of 0x0 will disable all events to the event log streaming FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the following in the AXI Performance Monitor:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the following in the AXI Performance Monitor:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the sample interval for the sampled metric counters

briefdescription.para: This function disables the following in the AXI Performance Monitor:

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the Metric Counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u32 CounterNum, u16 RangeUpper, u16 RangeLower)
memberdef.name: XAxiPmon_SetLogEnableRanges

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Upper and Lower Ranges for specified Metric Counter Log Enable Register.Event Logging starts when corresponding Metric Counter value falls in between these ranges.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

is the Metric Counter number for which Ranges are to be assigned.Use the XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER* defines for the counter number in xaxipmon.h. The valid values are 0 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_0) to 9 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_9).
specifies the Upper limit in 32 bit Register
specifies the Lower limit in 32 bit Register

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Ranges of specified Metric Counter Log Enable Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

is the Metric Counter number for which Ranges are to be returned.Use the XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER* defines for the counter number in xaxipmon.h. The valid values are 0 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_0) to 9 (XAPM_METRIC_COUNTER_9).
specifies the user reference variable which returns the Upper Range Value of the specified Metric Counter Log Enable Register.
specifies the user reference variable which returns the Lower Range Value of the specified Metric Counter Log Enable Register.
is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables External trigger pulse so that Metric Counters can be started on external trigger pulse for a Slot.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the External trigger pulse used to start Metric Counters on external trigger pulse for a Slot.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables External trigger pulse for Event Log so that Event Logging can be started on external trigger pulse for a Slot.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the External trigger pulse used to start Event Log on external trigger pulse for a Slot.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns a name for a given Metric.

parameterdescription.para: is one of the Metric Sets. User has to use XAPM_METRIC_SET_* macros in xaxipmon.h for this parameter

briefdescription.para: This function sets Write ID in ID register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Write ID to be written in ID register.

simplesect.para: If ID filtering for write is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then WriteID is written to XAPM_ID_OFFSET or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of WriteID are written to XAPM_ID_OFFSET.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Read ID in ID register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Read ID to be written in ID register.

simplesect.para: If ID filtering for read is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then ReadId is written to XAPM_RID_OFFSET or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of ReadId are written to XAPM_ID_OFFSET.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Write ID in ID register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

simplesect.para: WriteId is the required Write ID in ID register.

None. If ID filtering for write is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then 32 bit XAPM_ID_OFFSET contents are returned or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of XAPM_ID_OFFSET register are returned.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Read ID in ID register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

simplesect.para: ReadId is the required Read ID in ID register.

None. If ID filtering for write is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then 32 bit XAPM_RID_OFFSET contents are returned or if it is 16 bit width then higher 16 bits of XAPM_ID_OFFSET register are returned.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Latency Start point to calculate write latency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ISSUE or XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ACCEPT in xaxipmon.h.

simplesect.para: Param can be 0 - XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ISSUE or 1 - XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ACCEPT

briefdescription.para: This function sets Latency End point to calculate write latency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is XAPM_LATENCY_LASTWR or XAPM_LATENCY_FIRSTWR in xaxipmon.h.

simplesect.para: Param can be 0 - XAPM_LATENCY_LASTWR or 1 - XAPM_LATENCY_FIRSTWR

briefdescription.para: This function sets Latency Start point to calculate read latency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ISSUE or XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ACCEPT in xaxipmon.h.

simplesect.para: Param can be 0 - XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ISSUE or 1 - XAPM_LATENCY_ADDR_ACCEPT

briefdescription.para: This function sets Latency End point to calculate read latency.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is XAPM_LATENCY_LASTRD or XAPM_LATENCY_FIRSTRD in xaxipmon.h.

simplesect.para: Param can be 0 - XAPM_LATENCY_LASTRD or 1 - XAPM_LATENCY_FIRSTRD

briefdescription.para: This function returns Write Latency Start point.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Write Latency End point.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns read Latency Start point.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Read Latency End point.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Write ID Mask in ID Mask register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Write ID mask to be written in ID register.

simplesect.para: If ID masking for write is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then WrMask is written to XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of WrMask are written to XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET.

briefdescription.para: This function sets Read ID Mask in ID Mask register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the Read ID mask to be written in ID Mask register.

simplesect.para: If ID masking for read is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then RdMask is written to XAPM_RIDMASK_OFFSET or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of RdMask are written to XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Write ID Mask in ID Mask register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

simplesect.para: WrMask is the required Write ID Mask in ID Mask register.

If ID masking for write is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then 32 bit XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET contents are returned or if it is 16 bit width then lower 16 bits of XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET register are returned.

briefdescription.para: This function returns Read ID Mask in ID Mask register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

simplesect.para: RdMask is the required Read ID Mask in ID Mask register.

If ID masking for read is of 32 bits(for Zynq MP APM) width then 32 bit XAPM_RIDMASK_OFFSET contents are returned or if it is 16 bit width then higher 16 bits of XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET register are returned.

detaileddescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.
The XAxiPmon_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xaxipmon.h for the definition of XAxiPmon_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

detaileddescription.para: Run a self-test on the driver/device.

The test
Resets the device,
Writes a value into the Range Registers of Incrementer 0 and reads it back for comparison.
Resets the device again.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the value read from the Range Register of Incrementer 0 is the same as the value written.

simplesect.para: This is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer to the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables the Global Interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables the Global Interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XAPM_IXR_* constants defined in xaxipmon_hw.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XAPM_IXR__* bits defined in xaxipmon_hw.h.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XAxiPmon_IntrEnable(XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

detaileddescription.para: Use the XAPM_IXR_* constants defined in xaxipmon_hw.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XAPM_IXR_* bits defined in xaxipmon_hw.h.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XAxiPmon_IntrEnable(XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This routine clears the specified interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is the mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. This mask is formed by OR'ing XAPM_IXR_* bits defined in xaxipmon_hw.h.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XAxiPmon_IntrClear(XAxiPmon *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: This routine returns the Interrupt Status Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the Global Clock Counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disbles the Global Clock Counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the specified flag in Flag Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is one of the XAPM_FLAG_* masks defined in xaxipmon.h

briefdescription.para: This function disables the specified flag in Flag Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.
is one of the XAPM_FLAG_* masks defined in xaxipmon.h*

briefdescription.para: This function loads the sample interval register value into the sample interval counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This enables the down count of the sample interval counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This disables the down count of the sample interval counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This enables Reset of Metric Counters when Sample Interval Counter lapses.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This disables the down count of the sample interval counter.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables the ID Filter Masking.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disbles the ID Filter masking.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

briefdescription.para: This function samples Metric Counters to Sampled Metric Counters by reading Sample Register and also returns interval.

detaileddescription.para: i.e. the number of clocks in between previous read to the current read of sample register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XAxiPmon instance.

simplesect.para: Interval. i.e. the number of clocks in between previous read to the current read of sample register.

briefdescription.para: Read a register of the AXI Performance Monitor device.

detaileddescription.para: This macro provides register access to all registers using the register offsets defined above.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
is the offset of the register to read.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: u32 XAxiPmon_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: Write a register of the AXI Performance Monitor device.

detaileddescription.para: This macro provides register access to all registers using the register offsets defined above.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device.
is the offset of the register to write.
is the value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style Signature: void XAxiPmon_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset,u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: This file contains the driver API functions that can be used to access the AXI Performance Monitor device.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the XAxiPmon driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains a diagnostic self test function for the XAxiPmon driver.
This header file contains identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the AXI Performance Monitor.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of AxiMon devices in the system.
Refer to the xaxipmon.h header file for more information about this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bss 02/27/12 First release 2.00a bss 06/23/12 Updated to support v2_00a version of IP. 3.00a bss 09/03/12 Deleted XAxiPmon_SetAgent, XAxiPmon_GetAgent APIs and modified XAxiPmon_SetMetrics, XAxiPmon_GetMetrics APIs to support v2_01a version of IP. 3.01a bss 10/25/12 Added XAxiPmon_StartCounters and XAxiPmon_StopCounters APIs (CR #683799). Added XAxiPmon_StartEventLog and XAxiPmon_StopEventLog APIs (CR #683801). Added XAxiPmon_GetMetricName API (CR #683803). Modified XAxiPmon_SetMetrics and XAxiPmon_GetMetrics (CR #683746) Added XAxiPmon_EnableEventLog, XAxiPmon_DisableMetricsCounter, XAxiPmon_EnableMetricsCounter APIs to replace macros. Added XAxiPmon_SetMetricCounterCutOff, XAxiPmon_GetMetricCounterCutOff, XAxiPmon_EnableExternalTrigger and XAxiPmon_DisableExternalTrigger APIs to support new version of IP. 4.00a bss 01/17/13 To support new version of IP: Added XAxiPmon_SetLogEnableRanges, XAxiPmon_GetLogEnableRanges, XAxiPmon_EnableMetricCounterTrigger, XAxiPmon_DisableMetricCounterTrigger, XAxiPmon_EnableEventLogTrigger, XAxiPmon_DisableEventLogTrigger, XAxiPmon_SetWriteLatencyId, XAxiPmon_SetReadLatencyId, XAxiPmon_GetWriteLatencyId, XAxiPmon_GetReadLatencyId APIs and removed XAxiPmon_SetMetricCounterCutOff, XAxiPmon_GetMetricCounterCutOff, XAxiPmon_EnableExternalTrigger and XAxiPmon_DisableExternalTrigger APIs 5.00a bss 08/26/13 To support new version of IP: Modified XAxiPmon_CfgInitialize to add Mode of APM and ScaleFactor parameter. Modified Assert functions depending on Mode. Modified XAxiPmon_GetMetricCounter and XAxiPmon_GetSampledMetricCounter to include new Counters. Modified XAxiPmon_SetSampleInterval and XAxiPmon_GetSampleInterval to remove higher 32 bit value of SampleInterval since Sample Interval Register is only 32 bit. Added XAxiPmon_SetWrLatencyStart, XAxiPmon_SetWrLatencyEnd, XAxiPmon_SetRdLatencyStart XAxiPmon_SetRdLatencyEnd, XAxiPmon_GetWrLatencyStart, XAxiPmon_GetWrLatencyEnd, XAxiPmon_GetRdLatencyStart, XAxiPmon_GetRdLatencyEnd, XAxiPmon_SetWriteIdMask, XAxiPmon_SetReadIdMask, XAxiPmon_GetWriteIdMask and XAxiPmon_GetReadIdMask APIs. Renamed: XAxiPmon_SetWriteLatencyId to XAxiPmon_SetWriteId XAxiPmon_SetReadLatencyId to XAxiPmon_SetReadId XAxiPmon_GetWriteLatencyId to XAxiPmon_GetWriteId XAxiPmon_SetReadLatencyId to XAxiPmon_GetReadId. 6.2 bss 04/21/14 Updated XAxiPmon_CfgInitialize to Reset counters and FIFOs based on Modes(CR#782671). And if both profile and trace modes are present set mode as Advanced. 6.2 bss 03/02/15 Updated XAxiPmon_SetWriteId, XAxiPmon_SetReadId, XAxiPmon_GetWriteId, XAxiPmon_GetReadId XAxiPmon_SetWriteIdMask, XAxiPmon_SetReadIdMask, XAxiPmon_GetWriteIdMask, XAxiPmon_GetReadIdMask functions to support Zynq MP APM. 6.3 kvn 07/02/15 Modified code according to MISRA-C:2012 guidelines. 6.4 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XAxiPmon_CfgInitialize API. 6.7 sd 17/11/18 Fix Doxygen reported warnings 6.10 ht 06/23/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 6.12 ht 12/01/24 Fix GCC warnings

detaileddescription.para: See xaxipmon.h for more information about this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bss 02/22/12 First release 2.00a bss 06/23/12 Updated to support v2_00a version of IP.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the device specification for more information about this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bss 02/27/12 First release 2.00a bss 06/23/12 Updated to support v2_00a version of IP. 3.00a bss 09/03/12 Deleted XAPM_AGENT_OFFSET Macro to support v2_01a version of IP. 3.01a bss 10/25/12 To support new version of IP: Added XAPM_MCXLOGEN_OFFSET and XAPM_CR_EXTERNAL_TRIGGER_MASK macros. 4.00a bss 01/17/13 To support new version of IP: Added XAPM_LATENCYID_OFFSET, XAPM_CR_EVTLOG_EXTTRIGGER_MASK, XAPM_LATENCYID_RID_MASK and XAPM_LATENCYID_WID_MASK 5.00a bss 08/26/13 To support new version of IP: Added Macros XAPM_MC10_OFFSET to XAPM_MC47_OFFSET, XAPM_SMC10_OFFSET to XAPM_SMC47_OFFSET. Added macro XAPM_IDMASK_OFFSET, XAPM_SR_OFFSET. Added XAPM_CR_IDFILTER_ENABLE_MASK, XAPM_CR_WRLATENCY_START_MASK, XAPM_CR_WRLATENCY_END_MASK, XAPM_CR_RDLATENCY_START_MASK, XAPM_CR_RDLATENCY_END_MASK, XAPM_MASKID_RID_MASK and XAPM_MASKID_WID_MASK macros. Renamed: XAPM_LATENCYID_OFFSET to XAPM_ID_OFFSET, XAPM_LATENCYID_RID_MASK to XAPM_ID_RID_MASK, XAPM_LATENCYID_WID_MASK to XAPM_ID_WID_MASK.
6.2 bss 03/02/15 Added XAPM_RID_OFFSET and XAPM_RIDMASK_OFFSET to support Zynq MP APM.
6.3 kvn 07/02/15 Modified code according to MISRA-C:2012 guidelines.

detaileddescription.para: The self test function does a simple read/write test of the Alarm Threshold Register.
See XAxiPmon.h for more information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bss 02/24/12 First release 2.00a bss 06/23/12 Updated to support v2_00a version of IP. 6.3 kvn 07/02/15 Modified code according to MISRA-C:2012 guidelines.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a bss 02/27/12 First release 2.00a bss 06/23/12 Updated to support v2_00a version of IP. 6.3 kvn 07/02/15 Modified code according to MISRA-C:2012 guidelines. 6.10 ht 06/23/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 6.14 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
